text:
Once upon a time, in a small town surrounded by mountains, there was a peculiar old bookstore that nobody knew how to keep open. It stood at the crossroads of Main Street, with a weathered sign dangling outside: "Where Knowledge Finds Romance."

Every Saturday, an old librarian named Eloise would sweep the floors and tidy up. One foggy Saturday morning, as she dusted a bookshelf, a mysterious man stepped through the door. His eyes glinted beneath a black hat.

"Why do you keep this place going?" he asked.

Eloise smiled sadly. "I don't know how, but I do."

The man pulled out an old brass keychain. "Let me show you something."

She followed him out into the hallway, past shelves lined with dusty tomes. To her left was a dusty portrait of a man with a monocle. "That's mine," he said. "Sir Edgar."
